member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetColorsByMaterial.ColorOptionDto
member.summary:
Public-API shape for a ColorOption available to a chosen Material. Family sent as a string for FRONT readability; surcharge in int minor plus display string so the SPA never does currency math.
The ral-custom placeholder slug is filtered out of the list endpoint (it's not browseable) — the FRONT triggers it via the RAL palette modal and passes hex + code in the price request.

Public-API shape for a ColorOption available to a chosen Material. Family sent as a string for FRONT readability; surcharge in int minor plus display string so the SPA never does currency math.
The ral-custom placeholder slug is filtered out of the list endpoint (it's not browseable) — the FRONT triggers it via the RAL palette modal and passes hex + code in the price request.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetColorsByMaterial.IColorOptionReader
member.summary: Reader for color-option catalog data. excludes the ral-custom placeholder slug from the public list (it's modal-only, not browseable). returns the FULL set including ral-custom so the pricing pipeline can resolve it when the request body picks it.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetGlassTypesByMaterial.IGlassTypeReader
member.summary: Reader for glass-type catalog data. List variant returns the per-material compatible set ordered for display (default first, then SortOrder). LoadById variants are used by the pricing handler to hand the calculator the dictionary it needs for per-pane glass resolution.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetGlassTypesByMaterial.IGlassTypeReader.LoadDomainByMaterialAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the domain entities (not DTOs) for the glass types compatible with the given material. Used by the pricing pipeline so the calculator can resolve Guid.Empty → IsDefault and price per pane.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetGlassTypesByMaterial.GlassTypeDto
member.summary:
Public-API shape for a GlassType available to a chosen Material. Wire-stable; surcharge sent as int minor units plus an explicit display string so the SPA never does decimal math on currency values (per ADR-0002).
is the per-material baseline package — the FRONT auto-selects this on Step 5 entry so the price preview is non-empty immediately.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetGlassTypesByMaterial.GlassTypeDto.#ctor(System.Guid,System.String,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String,System.String,System.Decimal,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Public-API shape for a GlassType available to a chosen Material. Wire-stable; surcharge sent as int minor units plus an explicit display string so the SPA never does decimal math on currency values (per ADR-0002).
is the per-material baseline package — the FRONT auto-selects this on Step 5 entry so the price preview is non-empty immediately.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetHandleStyles.IHandleStyleReader
member.summary: Reader for handle-style catalog data. returns the per-material compat set ordered by SortOrder + default flag; + back the AccessoryCatalog bag the pricing pipeline uses.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetMaterialsByProductType.IMaterialReader.GetByIdAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Loads a single material's domain shape (not the DTO) — used by the pricing endpoint where we need BasePricePerSqmMinor + ProductTypeId for cross-field validation.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetMaterialsByProductType.IProductTypeExistsCheck
member.summary: Narrow existence check so the materials handler doesn't pull the full IProductTypeReader contract just to assert a foreign key.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetMaterialsByProductType.MaterialDto
member.summary: Public-API shape for a Material. Wire-stable — changes are breaking for codegen. Family + ThermalRating sent as strings (camelCase) for FRONT readability; price kept in minor units (tetri) with an explicit display string so the client never has to do its own decimal math on currency values.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetMaterialsByProductType.MaterialDto.#ctor(System.Guid,System.Guid,System.String,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,System.String,System.String,System.Int64,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Public-API shape for a Material. Wire-stable — changes are breaking for codegen. Family + ThermalRating sent as strings (camelCase) for FRONT readability; price kept in minor units (tetri) with an explicit display string so the client never has to do its own decimal math on currency values.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypeDetail.GetProductTypeDetailQuery
member.summary: Loads a single product type by id OR slug. The endpoint detects which from the route param shape (a parseable Guid → by-id; otherwise → by-slug). Returns Result<ProductTypeDetailDto> with the full constraints payload.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypeDetail.GetProductTypeDetailQuery.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Loads a single product type by id OR slug. The endpoint detects which from the route param shape (a parseable Guid → by-id; otherwise → by-slug). Returns Result<ProductTypeDetailDto> with the full constraints payload.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypeDetail.ProductTypeDetailDto
member.summary: Detail shape for a single ProductType — superset of the list DTO with dimension constraints attached. Used by the FRONT to populate Step 3's width/height bounds and by future detail pages.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypeDetail.ProductTypeDetailDto.#ctor(System.Guid,System.String,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,System.String,System.Int32,BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypeDetail.DimensionConstraintsDto)
member.summary: Detail shape for a single ProductType — superset of the list DTO with dimension constraints attached. Used by the FRONT to populate Step 3's width/height bounds and by future detail pages.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.IProductTypeReader
member.summary: Read-side abstraction for catalog product types. Implemented by Infrastructure using Dapper. Kept narrow so handlers don't take a dependency on a generic repository.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.IProductTypeReader.ListActiveAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Wire-shape DTO list for the public catalog endpoint.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.IProductTypeReader.GetByIdAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Loads the full domain entity by id — for handlers that need fields like dimension constraints that the list DTO doesn't carry.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.IProductTypeReader.GetBySlugAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Same shape as GetByIdAsync, but keyed by slug. Used by the detail endpoint when the FRONT navigates with a slug URL.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.GetProductTypesQuery
member.summary: Returns all active product types in display order. Hot read path — the handler routes through Dapper + a 5-minute IMemoryCache layer.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.ProductTypeDto
member.summary: Public-API shape for a catalog entry. Wire-stable — changes here are breaking changes for FRONT codegen.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.ProductTypeDto.#ctor(System.Guid,System.String,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Public-API shape for a catalog entry. Wire-stable — changes here are breaking changes for FRONT codegen.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Common.Abstractions.ICacheService.PingAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Round-trips a tiny value (set + get + remove) under a reserved key to verify the cache backend is alive. Used by the /health probe. Returns true on success, false if any step fails or returns an unexpected value.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Common.Abstractions.IStorageService.PingAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes a tiny probe file under a reserved key, reads it back, and deletes it. Used by the /health probe to verify the storage backend is writable. Returns true on success, false if any step fails.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Common.Abstractions.ITokenCipher
member.summary: Symmetric encrypt/decrypt for Meta access tokens. Application handlers stay PlainOldDotNet — they hand the cipher a string and a TTL, get back an they can persist. Implementation lives in Infrastructure and binds an AES-GCM key from configuration.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Common.Persistence.IBeqsanDbContext
member.summary: Abstraction over the EF Core DbContext used by Application handlers. DbSet<T> properties will be added here as Domain entities are introduced.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Common.Persistence.IBeqsanDbContext.PingAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Convenience alias for ; symmetric with ICacheService.PingAsync and IStorageService.PingAsync for the /health probe.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.AccessorySelectionInput
member.summary:
Wire shape for the configuration-level accessory bundle. Mirrors with the enums kept as strings the way the FRONT serialises them: sill.position: "Inner" | "Outer" | "Both" blind.control: "Manual" | "Electric" | "Remote"
Every field is optional — the customer can skip the whole step. Door product types must populate + when any pane opens; the validator enforces that with `reason: "door"` metadata.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.AccessorySelectionInput.#ctor(System.Nullable{System.Guid},System.Nullable{System.Guid},BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.SillSelectionInput,BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.BlindSelectionInput)
member.summary:
Wire shape for the configuration-level accessory bundle. Mirrors with the enums kept as strings the way the FRONT serialises them: sill.position: "Inner" | "Outer" | "Both" blind.control: "Manual" | "Electric" | "Remote"
Every field is optional — the customer can skip the whole step. Door product types must populate + when any pane opens; the validator enforces that with `reason: "door"` metadata.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.ColorSelectionInput
member.summary:
Wire shape for the configuration-level color decision. Mirrors with the hex/code pair carried as strings the way the FRONT picks them out of the RAL palette modal.
is required when this object is present. defaults to null (same as outer). + are required only when the outer option's slug is ral-custom.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.ColorSelectionInput.#ctor(System.Guid,System.Nullable{System.Guid},System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Wire shape for the configuration-level color decision. Mirrors with the hex/code pair carried as strings the way the FRONT picks them out of the RAL palette modal.
is required when this object is present. defaults to null (same as outer). + are required only when the outer option's slug is ral-custom.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.ComputePriceCommand
member.summary:
Configurator price input. The endpoint hits this command on every meaningful change in the FRONT (debounced).
is OPTIONAL: when omitted (null), the calculator synthesizes a single full-width Fixed pane so Step 2/3 request bodies (no panes field) keep producing their regression-canary numbers.

Configurator price input. The endpoint hits this command on every meaningful change in the FRONT (debounced).
is OPTIONAL: when omitted (null), the calculator synthesizes a single full-width Fixed pane so Step 2/3 request bodies (no panes field) keep producing their regression-canary numbers.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.ComputePriceValidator
member.summary: First-line input validation. Per-product-type rules (door taller than window, sliding requires 2+ panes, hinge matrix, etc.) are enforced inside against the actual ProductType row — this validator only catches the absolute outliers that would never make sense for any product (negative dims, > 8 m, pane with ratio outside (0, 1], missing position).

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.ConfigurationPaneInput
member.summary:
Wire shape for one pane in the price-request body. String-typed enums so the FRONT can author requests by hand and the API doc reads cleanly: openingType: "Fixed" | "Casement" | "Tilt" | "TiltAndTurn" | "Sliding" hingeSide: "Left" | "Right" | null glassExtras: ("LowECoating" | "Tempered" | "Frosted" | "Tinted")[] // distinct
is optional — when null or the handler resolves the material's default glass package so canaries from earlier slices keep their numbers without forcing the client to know about the new field.
defaults to an empty list when omitted. Duplicates are de-duped in the calculator; the validator rejects a Frosted + Tinted pair on the same pane (visual conflict).

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.ConfigurationPaneInput.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Decimal,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Nullable{System.Guid},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Boolean,System.String,System.String,System.Decimal)
member.summary:
Wire shape for one pane in the price-request body. String-typed enums so the FRONT can author requests by hand and the API doc reads cleanly: openingType: "Fixed" | "Casement" | "Tilt" | "TiltAndTurn" | "Sliding" hingeSide: "Left" | "Right" | null glassExtras: ("LowECoating" | "Tempered" | "Frosted" | "Tinted")[] // distinct
is optional — when null or the handler resolves the material's default glass package so canaries from earlier slices keep their numbers without forcing the client to know about the new field.
defaults to an empty list when omitted. Duplicates are de-duped in the calculator; the validator rejects a Frosted + Tinted pair on the same pane (visual conflict).

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.InstallationOptionInput
member.summary: Wire shape for the configurator's Step-8 installation decision. Region is a camelCase string that the handler parses defensively with Enum.TryParse<InstallationRegion>; invalid tokens flow back as a structured configurator.installation.regionInvalid error with metadata.got.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.InstallationOptionInput.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Wire shape for the configurator's Step-8 installation decision. Region is a camelCase string that the handler parses defensively with Enum.TryParse<InstallationRegion>; invalid tokens flow back as a structured configurator.installation.regionInvalid error with metadata.got.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.PriceBreakdownDto
member.summary: Wire shape for /configurator/price. AreaSqm is sent as a string ("1.68") so JS Number precision doesn't bite — same decision the FRONT made for MaterialDto.BasePricePerSqmDisplay. Money is paired (amountMinor + amountDisplay) so the client never does its own currency math.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.PriceBreakdownDto.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.PriceLineDto},System.Int64,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Wire shape for /configurator/price. AreaSqm is sent as a string ("1.68") so JS Number precision doesn't bite — same decision the FRONT made for MaterialDto.BasePricePerSqmDisplay. Money is paired (amountMinor + amountDisplay) so the client never does its own currency math.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.ReviewCommand
member.summary: Step-8 review request — same wire shape as plus an installation block, returning a richer grouped breakdown + delivery info. The FRONT calls /price for live recompute on earlier steps and switches to /review on Step 8 to render the full summary screen with grouped totals, warranty, and lead time.

Step-8 review request — same wire shape as plus an installation block, returning a richer grouped breakdown + delivery info. The FRONT calls /price for live recompute on earlier steps and switches to /review on Step 8 to render the full summary screen with grouped totals, warranty, and lead time.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.ReviewHandler
member.summary: Handler for the Step-8 review request. Delegates the price math to via MediatR — same canary numbers, same validator chain, no risk of drift — and layers three review- only outputs on top: 1. Grouped pricing breakdown — line codes sorted into Material / Glass / Color / Accessories / Installation buckets, VAT and grand total separated. 2. Lead time estimate via . 3. Warranty terms via .

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.ReviewHandler.#ctor(BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetProductTypes.IProductTypeReader,BEQSAN.Application.Catalog.GetLockTypes.ILockTypeReader,MediatR.ISender)
member.summary: Handler for the Step-8 review request. Delegates the price math to via MediatR — same canary numbers, same validator chain, no risk of drift — and layers three review- only outputs on top: 1. Grouped pricing breakdown — line codes sorted into Material / Glass / Color / Accessories / Installation buckets, VAT and grand total separated. 2. Lead time estimate via . 3. Warranty terms via .

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.ReviewHandler.GroupBreakdown(BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.ComputePrice.PriceBreakdownDto)
member.summary: Sorts the flat list into the 5 UX buckets the Step-8 receipt renders. VAT and grand total are returned separately because they're rendered below the disclosure group rows.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.ReviewResponseDto
member.summary: Rich response shape for the Step-8 review screen. Carries the flattened for power consumers AND a pre-grouped pricing structure the FRONT renders directly into the receipt-style summary without re-parsing line codes.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.ReviewResponseDto.#ctor(BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.PricingDto,BEQSAN.Application.Configurator.Review.DeliveryDto)
member.summary: Rich response shape for the Step-8 review screen. Carries the flattened for power consumers AND a pre-grouped pricing structure the FRONT renders directly into the receipt-style summary without re-parsing line codes.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Connect.CompleteConnectCommand
member.summary: Step 2 — Meta calls us back with `code` + `state`. We verify the state, exchange the code for a long-lived user token, discover the pages, and persist the account + page tokens encrypted.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Connect.CompleteConnectCommand.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Step 2 — Meta calls us back with `code` + `state`. We verify the state, exchange the code for a long-lived user token, discover the pages, and persist the account + page tokens encrypted.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Connect.StartConnectCommand
member.summary: Step 1 of the OAuth dance — admin clicks „Connect Facebook" and we mint a `state` nonce + return Meta's dialog URL. The state is cached briefly so we can verify it on callback.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.IAiAssistService
member.summary: Single AI service for caption drafting and reply suggestions. Backed by Claude Sonnet 4.6 — model id stays inside the implementation so swapping providers (Anthropic direct, KIE, Azure OpenAI) is one DI registration.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.IAiAssistService.DraftCaptionAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.param: Free-form admin brief — e.g. „ახალი მოდელი slim 70mm".
One of: friendly | technical | promo. Optional.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.IMetaGraphClient
member.summary: Authenticated Meta Graph operations on behalf of a single page. The caller passes the decrypted page-scoped token explicitly — this client doesn't touch persistence and doesn't decide which page is active.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.IMetaGraphClient.ListConversationsAsync(System.String,System.String,BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxChannel,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Page conversations (DMs) — pulled on demand or on a poll. The page-scoped token is required; messenger + IG share the endpoint via the `platform` query param.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.IMetaOAuthClient
member.summary: OAuth flow with Meta — build the consent URL, exchange the code, upgrade to long-lived, discover pages + IG business accounts. No persistence concerns: returns plain DTOs, the use-case handler turns them into Domain entities.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.IMetaOAuthClient.BuildAuthorizeUrl(System.String)
member.summary: Build the dialog URL the admin will be redirected to. is a CSRF nonce we generate and verify on the callback.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.IMetaOAuthClient.GetPagesAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: All pages the user has admin access to, with their page-scoped tokens and linked IG business account (if any).

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.ISocialAccountRepository
member.summary: Persistence boundary for + the pages it owns. Implementation lives in Infrastructure (EF Core, see ADR-0003). Application handlers never touch DbContext directly.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.MetaTokenResponse
member.summary: Result of exchanging an OAuth code for a long-lived token. is what Meta returns (typically ~5184000 = 60 days); the application layer turns that into an absolute UTC timestamp before persisting.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Contracts.MetaTokenResponse.#ctor(System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary: Result of exchanging an OAuth code for a long-lived token. is what Meta returns (typically ~5184000 = 60 days); the application layer turns that into an absolute UTC timestamp before persisting.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Posts.PublishPostCommand
member.summary: Composer submission. is one entry per (page, platform) the admin ticked — we fan out into N rows under a shared composer id and publish them sequentially so a partial failure leaves useful state (some published, some failed) rather than rolling back the world.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Posts.PublishPostCommand.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BEQSAN.Application.Social.Posts.PublishTarget})
member.summary: Composer submission. is one entry per (page, platform) the admin ticked — we fan out into N rows under a shared composer id and publish them sequentially so a partial failure leaves useful state (some published, some failed) rather than rolling back the world.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Webhooks.ReceiveMetaWebhookCommand
member.summary: Webhook fan-out from Meta. Payload is delivered as already-parsed records by the API edge — the handler doesn't see raw JSON. Idempotent on via .

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Application.Social.Webhooks.ReceiveMetaWebhookCommand.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BEQSAN.Application.Social.Webhooks.WebhookEvent})
member.summary: Webhook fan-out from Meta. Payload is delivered as already-parsed records by the API edge — the handler doesn't see raw JSON. Idempotent on via .
